attributes.@cm03: Solving one- and two-step linear equations; inverse operations; equality as balance (T1, T2). Fluency under pressure, not first instruction.
Converting between fractions, decimals, percents; percent change and discount; rate-time-distance; comparing rates (T3, T4, T5, T7, T8)
Integer operations with positive and negative numbers; rounding to specified place values (T6, T9, T10)

ops.insert: Operations & Algebraic Thinking — Compare & Order Computed Totals (K–2, Grade 1 anchor)

attributes.@cm03: Add and subtract within 20; compare and order computed expression totals by total value; develop fluency through repeated contextualised practice. Grade 1 anchor; K extension (within 5–10, addition only); Grade 2 extension (mixed +/–, within 20, longer sequences). Does NOT cover place-value comparison (1.NBT.B.3) — that is a separate slot.
attributes.@cm05: Snack Line Shuffle (K–2 anchor, Prototype P1 — GO as of 2026-04-08)
attributes.@cm06: CCSS.MATH.CONTENT.1.OA.C.6 — Add and subtract within 20; use strategies to develop fluency. Anchor only; game supports consolidation and practice, not first instruction. Standards explicitly excluded: 1.NBT.B.3 (overreach — not targeted).

ops.insert: K–2 (K–1 core within 10, addition-dominant; Grade 2+ extension with mixed +/–, within 20, longer sequences)

1.OA.C.6 is the first standard to explicitly call for within-20 fluency as a goal. Ordering computed totals is a natural fluency consolidation context at Grade 1. Kindergarten entry uses addition within 5 only (warm-up tier). Grade 2 extension introduces subtraction pairs and longer sequences.

CCSS.MATH.CONTENT.1.OA.C.6 — Add and subtract within 20, demonstrating fluency for addition and subtraction within 10. Use strategies such as making ten, decomposing a number leading to a ten, and creating equivalent but easier sums.
1.NBT.B.3 (place-value comparison using >, =, < notation). Removed as overreach on 2026-04-08. Do not include in any educator-facing alignment materials for this game.
Practice and consolidation. Not a first-instruction tool. Intended for use after classroom introduction of addition strategies. Complements but does not replace direct instruction on 1.OA.C.6.
Operations and Algebraic Thinking (OA). Does NOT sit under Number and Operations in Base Ten (NBT) despite surface-level similarity to comparison tasks.
Sequence / Ordering — first K-2 member of this family. Family registry: Game Family Registry (project: N9S2kjQdv3s7tyya), Sequence / Ordering entry.

Bakery Rush (Exact-Sum Composition family) occupies the "Addition & Subtraction to 20 — Compose to Exact Total" slot. Snack Line Shuffle occupies the distinct "Compare and Order Computed Totals" sub-skill. The two games serve different cognitive targets within 1.OA and should both appear in a K-2 portfolio without overlap risk.

M1 bigger addend = bigger total; M2 equal totals must rank differently; M3 subtraction always loses; M4 leftmost digit dominance; M5 operation sign blindness; M6 zero-subtraction overgeneralisation; M7 bigger number first = bigger result. Full register: Misconception Library (project: cyt3zvpjf32D1Ddt). M1–M3 active in K-1 levels; M4–M7 gated to Grade 2+ levels.
Addition within 5 only · 2–3 kids · largest-first rule · all totals distinct · no subtraction. CCSS: K.OA.A.1, K.OA.A.2 (informal practice, not alignment claim).
Addition within 10 · 3–4 kids · largest-first rule · contrast sets targeting M1 · no subtraction. CCSS: 1.OA.C.6 (anchor standard).
Grade 2 tier (gated — not in P1 build):

Mixed +/– within 20 · 5–6 kids · ordering rule variants (largest-first, smallest-first, positional constraints) · M3–M7 misconception sets active. CCSS: 2.OA.B.2 (extension, not anchor). Gate: content plan must be reviewed by Curriculum Architect before any Grade 2 level is authored.
6-kid mixed +/– lines within 20 under positional constraints. Beyond this, multi-step or unknown-addend expressions require a different UI model. The game has no content pathway into Grades 3+.

Full P1 build brief — Snack Line Shuffle entry in project 9bfNR2acXuAHiWyC (Prototype Specifications), under "Approved — Ready to Build / Building". Covers scene spec, Option B Serve mechanic, content constraints, grade-band gate, and all 14 P1 DoD gates.

Sequence / Ordering family entry in project N9S2kjQdv3s7tyya (Game Family Registry). Family upgraded to Well Covered on 2026-04-08. Snack Line Shuffle listed as first K-2 member; Probability Pipeline as Grades 5-6 member (REVISE status).
M1–M7 registered in project cyt3zvpjf32D1Ddt (Misconception Library). M4+M7 co-occurrence flagged. M1–M3 active at K-1 levels; M4–M7 gated to Grade 2+ levels.
Snack Line Shuffle in project LnpYq2qGt5DrXpda (Game Concepts Pipeline). Pipeline status: GO — Prototype P1 (advanced 2026-04-08).

"Addition & Subtraction to 20 (K–2)" — task a1111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111104 in this project. That slot is anchored to Bakery Rush (Exact-Sum Composition). These two slots serve complementary but non-overlapping cognitive targets within the 1.OA domain.

Expected value of simple probability models; outcome-weighted probability computation (T12)
Number sense: counting to 120, comparing quantities

Understanding fractions as parts of a whole

Letter-sound correspondence, blending, sight words
Context clues, morphology, tier 2 vocabulary
Plant/animal life cycles, food chains, habitats
Gravity, friction, balanced/unbalanced forces
Division as partitioning and grouping; ceiling division for real-world allocation
Solving for unknown variables; inverse operations; balance equations
Multi-step unit rates; chaining t=d/r across legs; evaluating T ≤ T_deadline and stops ≥ N inequalities
